verses.text: God bids Jonah cry against Nineveh, but he rises 'to flee... from the presence of the LORD' to Tarshish. Flight from duty. A reluctant prophet runs from the call he dislikes.
God bids Jonah cry against Nineveh, but he rises 'to flee... from the presence of the LORD' to Tarshish. Flight from duty. A reluctant prophet runs from the call he dislikes.
God bids Jonah cry against Nineveh, but he rises 'to flee... from the presence of the LORD' to Tarshish. Flight from duty. A reluctant prophet runs from the call he dislikes.

God sends a great storm; the heathen sailors pray each to his god, while Jonah sleeps, till the shipmaster rouses him. Asleep in danger. The disobedient prophet is less awake than the pagans around him.

God sends a great storm; the heathen sailors pray each to his god, while Jonah sleeps, till the shipmaster rouses him. Asleep in danger. The disobedient prophet is less awake than the pagans around him.

God sends a great storm; the heathen sailors pray each to his god, while Jonah sleeps, till the shipmaster rouses him. Asleep in danger. The disobedient prophet is less awake than the pagans around him.
The lot falls on Jonah; he confesses, 'I am an Hebrew; and I fear the LORD... which hath made the sea and the dry land.' Owned at last. Even in flight he must confess the God he serves.
The lot falls on Jonah; he confesses, 'I am an Hebrew; and I fear the LORD... which hath made the sea and the dry land.' Owned at last. Even in flight he must confess the God he serves.
The lot falls on Jonah; he confesses, 'I am an Hebrew; and I fear the LORD... which hath made the sea and the dry land.' Owned at last. Even in flight he must confess the God he serves.
The men are afraid; Jonah tells them to cast him into the sea, owning the tempest is for his sake. Honest sacrifice. He will not let others perish for his sin.
The men are afraid; Jonah tells them to cast him into the sea, owning the tempest is for his sake. Honest sacrifice. He will not let others perish for his sin.
The men are afraid; Jonah tells them to cast him into the sea, owning the tempest is for his sake. Honest sacrifice. He will not let others perish for his sin.
They row hard for land but cannot, and cry that God lay not innocent blood on them. Reluctant mariners. The heathen show more tenderness than the prophet showed Nineveh.
They row hard for land but cannot, and cry that God lay not innocent blood on them. Reluctant mariners. The heathen show more tenderness than the prophet showed Nineveh.

They cast him out, the sea calms, and the men fear the LORD; 'the LORD had prepared a great fish' to swallow Jonah three days. Sovereign mercy. Even Jonah's punishment becomes the means of his rescue.
